prompt:
Photorealistic award-winning photograph of an old cramped tailor’s workshop, worn wooden floors, aged shelves, dusty air, scattered fabric bolts and sewing tools, warm golden sunlight coming through a single side window, realistic volumetric light, subtle rim lighting, background softly blurred and slightly darkened.

In the foreground, three mannequins arranged left to right to clearly show overfitting, good fit, and underfitting.

Left mannequin: overfitting.
A clearly lumpy, asymmetrical mannequin body with unnatural bulges, one uneven shoulder, a warped torso, and a protruding hip, like noisy irregular training data. It wears a dark bespoke suit that has been obsessively over-tailored to match every tiny irregularity: dense white chalk seam lines, hundreds of tiny silver pins, many darts and tucks, tightly pulled seams, visible tension in the fabric, and one seam beginning to tear at the shoulder. The tailoring should look physically real but excessive, brittle, and hyper-specific to this one distorted form.

Middle mannequin: good fit.
A perfectly symmetrical standard mannequin wearing a smooth, elegant navy blue suit with natural drape, balanced structure, and clean tailoring. It should look like the ideal generalized fit.

Right mannequin: underfitting.
A mannequin with a baggy, shapeless, poorly structured garment or unfinished draped fabric, clearly too simple and too loose, with minimal shaping and no refinement.

Foreground: wooden worktable, scissors, thread spools, measuring tape, fabric scraps, all in soft focus.
Depth of field: the three mannequins and garments are sharply in focus, while shelves and tools behind them blur naturally.
Strictly no text, no numbers, no labels, no logos, no symbols, no signage, no diagrams, no equations, no markings anywhere. Purely a real physical workshop photo.

Negative prompt:
text, letters, numbers, labels, logos, signage, watermark, infographic, chart, equation, diagram, arrows, captions, UI, screen, poster, illustration, painting, cartoon, surreal elements, extra mannequins, extra people, distorted faces, hands, mannequins with faces, mannequin text markings, floating objects, impossible stitching, plastic shine, overly glossy fabric, flat lighting, overexposed window, cluttered background, noisy background, low detail, blurry foreground, duplicated suits, identical mannequins, fashion runway, studio backdrop
metaphor: An obsessively over-tailored suit on a distorted mannequin represents overfitting to noisy data, contrasting against an elegant, properly draped suit for good fit and a shapeless garment for underfitting.
